plugins.name: pre-mortem
plugins.description: Multi-agent project pre-mortem based on Gary Klein's technique. Spawns parallel AI agents with different failure-finding mandates, synthesizes ranked risks with mitigations.

plugins.name: profile-me
plugins.description: Build a portable AI profile of the user from their digital footprint. Produces personal portraits, professional portraits, working-with-me guides, and a compact system prompt for any AI assistant.

plugins.name: getting-second-opinions
plugins.description: Use GitHub Copilot CLI to validate decisions with gpt-5.4-codex for critical code reviews, design decisions, and pre-merge validation.

plugins.name: handle-pr
plugins.description: Autonomously handle GitHub PR review comments: evaluate each thread, implement HIGH/MEDIUM changes, run tests, Copilot quality pass, commit, reply to every thread, and watch for follow-up comments for 12 minutes.

plugins.name: chezmoi-drift
plugins.description: Audit chezmoi-managed dotfiles for drift, unmanaged files worth tracking, and broken Claude/Codex shared-skill installs.

plugins.name: okta-sso-debugger
plugins.description: Diagnose, validate, and document Okta CIAM SSO configurations for Leap tenants. 22-check validator, user debugging workflow, trustClaims detection.

plugins.name: trust-audit
plugins.description: Audit whether a product will feel trustworthy, creepy, deceptive, risky, or surprisingly unsafe to users across permissions, privacy, billing, file mutation, and silent-failure surfaces.

plugins.name: support-inbox-simulation
plugins.description: Simulate support emails, reviews, refunds, and confused complaints a launch or feature change will generate, then identify the diagnostics and product fixes that cut founder-tax.

plugins.name: first-run-red-team
plugins.description: Red-team a product's onboarding, permissions, activation, and first-success experience to find where new users get confused, think the app is broken, or abandon setup.

metadata.description: Personal Claude skills by Carl Kibler.
